title: stanmarkia medialis
intro: Stanmarkia medialis, commonly known as the "Yellow Passion Flower," is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its potential to aid in the treatment of anxiety, insomnia, and inflammation. The health benefits of Stanmarkia medialis can be attributed to its therapeutic actions, including its ability to act as a sedative, anxiolytic, and anti-inflammatory agent. The bioactive constituents of the plant, which include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, are responsible for its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
side_effects: Stanmarkia medialis can have side effects if used improperly, such as severe allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. Prolonged use of the plant can also lead to liver damage and kidney failure, as well as an increased risk of bleeding and bruising due to its anticoagulant properties. In some cases, consuming the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, often accompanied by headaches and dizziness. If ingested in large quantities or over an extended period, Stanmarkia medialis can even lead to seizures, coma, or even death in extreme cases.
